'Jessi simply loved covering the Wild and hockey throughout Minnesota and had a way of brightening everyone's day with her upbeat, bubbly personality. I have literally NEVER met anybody that had a way of being EVERYBODY's friend.

'More than anything, she absolutely loved Hudson, Cayden and Avery and was the greatest mother who did everything she could to bring joy to her sweetest kids. Even at Friday's practice in her beloved Iowa State sweatshirt, she was so excited to take them to the seasonal opening of Cup and Cone in WBL. Seeing those precious pics yesterday, she provided them with another incredibly fun and loving day.

'My heart breaks for Mike for the loss of his wife and children and Jessi's family, friends and colleagues for this unimaginable loss. This hockey community lost a wonderful, energetic and one of a kind voice and story teller and Grade A person.

'Please keep Jessi and her beautiful kids in your thoughts and prayers. I wish Mike all the peace and support and happiness in the world.'

Pierce is pictured with her children while adorned in Team USA hockey apparel

The 37-year-old mother of three loved her children 'more than anything', an ex-colleague said

As pictured by Fox 9, Pierce's home is seen after local firefighters extinguished the blaze

The tragedy unfolded in the early hours of Saturday morning.

The fire department responded to a 911 call in White Bear Lake just before 5:30am Saturday from Pierce's neighbors, who saw flames coming through the roof and said there were likely people inside.

It is believed they found a 'fully involved structure fire' upon arrival.

'Our hearts ache for those involved in this tragedy,' Chief Peterson said in a statement. 'We ask for the opportunity to allow our community to come together and support one another during this difficult time.'

The Minnesota Wild issued a team statement after Pierce's death was confirmed.

'The Minnesota Wild organization is heartbroken by the tragic loss of Jessi Pierce and her children,' the Wild said in the statement.



'Jessi was a kind, compassionate person that cared deeply about her family and those around her. She served as a dedicated ambassador for the game of hockey during her time covering the Wild and the NHL. Jessi and her children will be greatly missed.'

Sports.title: Carl Froch provides John Fury fight update amid bitter feud
Sports.content:
Carl Froch provides John Fury fight update amid bitter feud

John Fury, father of Tyson, has relentlessly called out former world champion Carl Froch but talks over a fight have broken down

View Image Carl Froch had accepted a fight with John Fury (Image: PA)

Carl Froch has claimed John Fury has gone quiet after calling him out for a fight.

‌



Fury, father of former heavyweight world champion, confronted Froch last month ahead of his son's press conference to confirm his comeback fight against Arslanbek Makhmudov. Froch agreed to fight the 60-year-old on the undercard at the Tottenham Hotspur Stadium on April 11 but now claims his rival has backed down.

‌



"What level was John Fury? He had a couple of fights... I'm not sure how many he had, but he was a journeyman," he told NewBettingSites.uk. He's been brave enough to get in there and have a go. I think he got splattered. So, he's a very low-level journeyman. But still brave enough to get in there, still fought, still laced the gloves up, so he deserves credit for that. But he was absolutely fucking useless.

‌



George Liddard completes half marathon hours after title defence READ MORE :

Tyson Fury hands Anthony Joshua major boost in pursuit of heavyweight fight READ MORE :

FOLLOW OUR FIGHTING FB PAGE! Latest boxing and MMA news, analysis and much more via our dedicated Facebook page

"John Fury has gone quiet on our fight, as he does. You don't want any of the cobra. He doesn't need me punching him around the ribs. I physically wouldn't hit him with a right hand on the chin if I boxed him. I wouldn't do it, just out of respect. I’d just jab him in the face. My jab has not even half the power on my right-hand. And I'd only work his body. I'd only hit him in the bread basket. That's all I'd need to do. So I'd jab him in the face, and work the body."

Article continues below

Froch also suggested Fury would have an easier time if he took on Jake Paul. "Jake Paul vs. John Fury in his prime would have been an even match," he added. "Both operated at a similar level. John Fury in his day would probably beat Jake Paul because he knows how to throw a punch, but it would have been a close fight. "

Sports.title: Ex-jockey caught on CCTV carrying out petrol station smash and grab jailed for six years
Sports.content:
Ex-jockey caught on CCTV carrying out petrol station smash and grab jailed for six years

Mark Aghan pleaded guilty to two counts of theft, one count of arson and another count of serious criminal trespass after reversing a utility truck into the service station building to steal from the till

View 3 Images A utility truck was reversed into the service station building (Image: @adelaidenow)

A former jockey who was caught on CCTV carrying out a smash and grab raid on a petrol station with a utility truck has been jailed for six years. Mark Aghan, 65, pleaded guilty to two counts of theft, one count of arson and another count of serious criminal trespass in a non-residential building in March and April 2024.

‌



The offences were outlined when Aghan appeared in court in Adelaide, Australia, for sentencing. According to His Honour Judge White, Aghan took a utility truck which had been left for fixing at the service station where his girlfriend worked and used it to reverse into the locked doors of the station building, entering the premises and stealing £260 from the till.

‌



Staff left shocked after racehorse wanders into Newmarket hotel READ MORE :

Harry Redknapp's star horse The Jukebox Man being treated for mystery muscle injury READ MORE :

This incident was captured on CCTV after which Aghan drove the vehicle away and set it alight. Following his arrest he claimed the truck was missing.

‌



The court was told that Aghan had a long record of offending and had spent time in prison since he retired from a 20 year career as a jockey.

Detailing Aghan’s past, the judge said as a student he had obtained a degree in mechanical engineering and went on to become a well-known jockey, riding in Sydney and overseas.

View 3 Images Mark Aghan enters the building after smashing through the doors (Image: @adelaidenow)

‌



“In 1994 you had a career-ending fall and, unfortunately for you, a life of crime took off from that point,” Judge White said.

He met his partner and co-offender Lisa-Ann Smith, when he moved to the remote town of Coober Pedy in South Australia in 2000, where they acted as “a drug-addicted couple”.

“You are now 65 with a bad health record, including pancreatic cancer in 1999 from which you remarkably recovered, unlike many others with that same awful disease,” said the judge. "You also have heart issues.”

‌



View 3 Images Aghan was filmed removing the till before making off with £260 (Image: @adelaidenow)

He continued: “I accept your pleas as a sign of remorse and contrition. You are now not affected by drugs and do not want to be in the future. You may be tempted to return to that lifestyle.

“However I have taken a view that you must be sick of crime and drugs, given what it has done to your once productive life. You are obviously intelligent, as your obtained degrees in engineering and science indicates.”

Article continues below

The judge sentenced Aghan to a total of six years imprisonment and, after applying a discount for his early guilty pleas and time in custody, this was reduced to five years, eight months and eight days.

Sports.title: Pep Guardiola stops three Arsenal stars as tunnel footage emerges after Carabao Cup final
Sports.content:
Pep Guardiola stops three Arsenal stars as tunnel footage emerges after Carabao Cup final

Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola shared warm exchanges with Bukayo Saka, Eberechi Eze and Gabriel Jesus in the Wembley tunnel following his side's Carabao Cup final victory over Arsenal

View 2 Images Pep Guardiola stopped three Arsenal players in the tunnel at Wembley (Image: Paramount)

Pep Guardiola enjoyed a cordial moment with Bukayo Saka, Eberechi Eze and Gabriel Jesus following Manchester City's Carabao Cup final triumph over Arsenal. The Catalan was in jubilant spirits after his team defeated Mikel Arteta's side beneath the Wembley arch.

‌



Nico O'Reilly's double header proved sufficient to deliver Guardiola's fifth League Cup success. For Arteta, it represented a laboured display which concluded Arsenal's pursuit of an historic quadruple.

‌



Following the final whistle, Guardiola encountered Saka, Eze and Jesus in the tunnel. He exchanged a warm embrace with the trio, clasping hands with Saka and hugging Eze, who was absent from the final due to a calf problem.

‌



England's 2026 World Cup kits This article contains affiliate links, we will receive a commission on any sales we generate from it. Learn more Various Prices England Official Store Buy Now on England Official Store Product Description England and Nike have launched the new home, away and goalkeeper kits to be worn at this summer's FIFA World Cup. You can get free delivery on all orders with the code: ENGFREEDEL

Guardiola also engaged in conversation with Jesus, the very player he offloaded to the Gunners during the summer of 2022. Their interaction seemed warm and amicable, with Eze grinning throughout their discussion.

Unsurprisingly, nobody in the capital wore a broader smile than Guardiola. His team entered the Wembley showdown as outsiders, yet they comprehensively outperformed the sluggish Gunners

‌



FOLLOW OUR ARSENAL FB PAGE! Latest Gunners news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

For Arteta, it represents a significant setback which prolongs his barren spell without silverware. Whilst Arsenal have threatened to claim the Premier League over the past four campaigns, Arteta remains without a major honour since lifting the 2020 FA Cup shortly after succeeding Unai Emery.

Arsenal remain in the FA Cup and the Champions League, and also lead the Premier League by nine points, but the League Cup final represented a significant chance to break their trophy drought. Following the match, Arteta encouraged supporters to focus on the broader, more optimistic outlook, despite the underwhelming performance.

‌



View 2 Images Guardiola was the happier of the two managers after the game (Image: GLYN KIRK, AFP via Getty Images)

When asked what he would tell supporters after the frustrating loss, he said: "Well, especially to have some perspective on it. What this team has done in the last eight months has been incredible.

"We're going to use this disappointment and this fire in the belly to have the most amazing two months that we have ever together. That's on us and we'll manage that energy in the right way. Now we have to go through that pain and disappointment and it's normal and it's part of football.

‌



Content cannot be displayed without consent

"For sure, there will be a lot of aspects that we will discuss. There will be a lot of aspects that we need to discuss, I think we have to reflect on that, bring the temperature down a little bit and we will discuss that."

Following the international break, Arsenal head to Southampton for the FA Cup quarter-final on 4 April, before jetting off to Portugal to face Sporting in the first leg of their Champions League quarter-final tie on 7 April.

Article continues below

Sports.title: Zinedine Zidane reaches 'verbal agreement' over new job after Man Utd links
Sports.content:
Zinedine Zidane reaches 'verbal agreement' over new job after Man Utd links

Zinedine Zidane has long been linked with a move to Manchester United but it appears that the Frenchman will not be moving to Old Trafford after all with a different job now on offer

View 2 Images Zinedine Zidane is set to return to management (Image: Getty Images)

Zinedine Zidane is set to become France’s new manager, with an appointment seemingly close. The former Real Madrid and Juventus star has been out of work since leaving the Bernabeu for the second time in 2021.

‌



He has regularly been linked with taking the reins at Manchester United, while the French national team have long admired him.

‌



And it appears that Zidane could finally be set to return to the dugout over the coming months. According to Le Parisien, the 53-year-old has reached a verbal agreement with the nation to take the reins from Didier Deschamps after this summer’s World Cup.

‌



Giving an update on the search for a new boss, French Football Federation (FFF) president Philippe Diallo suggested that an announcement could be made soon. Speaking in an interview Le Figaro, he said: "Yes, I know his name."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Marcus Rashford knows all about Trent Alexander-Arnold punishment after Real Madrid decision READ MORE :

Jamie Carragher tells Tottenham to sack Igor Tudor NOW after Nottingham Forest thrashing READ MORE :

Diallo added: "It takes a profile that ticks many boxes and which can also be the subject of support from the French people, since this French football team is the team of the French people.

‌



The FFF chief also confirmed that they had received "fewer than five applications," with each of those interested in the role being French. France will take on Norway, Senegal and the winners of an Inter-Confederation playoff at this summer's World Cup.

Zidane had previously hinted that he was closing in on a return to management earlier this season. He said: “It will happen soon. Very soon.”

View 2 Images Zidane is set to replace Didier Deschamps after the World Cup (Image: Getty Images)

‌



Zidane added: "I’d like to be the head coach of the France team one day. I will surely return to coaching."

One of Zidane’s former teammates Nicolas Anelka had previously given his take on him taking the reins with France and suggested that he would be an excellent appointment.

He said: "I don't know if it's official, but I think it will be Zidane. Everyone says it will be him, and he's the best choice; he proved it at Real Madrid. He was a good coach. He will be a good leader for the French players."

Article continues below

While Zidane is due to return to management over the coming months, United still have a decision to make over the man in the Old Trafford dugout. Michael Carrick was appointed as Ruben Amorim’s successor earlier this season and he has impressed with seven wins from ten games in the Premier League - leaving the Red Devils sitting third in the table heading into the final international break of the campaign.

Sports.title: Feyenoord boss Robin van Persie gives brutal assessment of Raheem Sterling after hauling him off
Sports.content:
Feyenoord boss Robin van Persie gives brutal assessment of Raheem Sterling after hauling him off

Raheem Sterling failed to make an impact in Feyenoord's underwhelming 1-1 draw against Ajax on Sunday and was substituted in the 55th minute by manager Robin van Persie

View 2 Images Raheem Sterling failed to make an impact for Feyenoord vs Ajax (Image: Wart Brinkerhof/Marcel ter Bals/Getty Images)

Robin van Persie hinted that he is running out of patience with Raheem Sterling after hauling him off early in Feyenoord's draw against Ajax on Sunday. Sterling joined the Dutch side on a free transfer in January and is still finding his feet a few months later.

‌



The 31-year-old winger moved to Feyenoord in search of a fresh start after finding himself frozen out at Chelsea. He mutually agreed to terminate his contract with Chelsea in order to get back onto the pitch after a frustrating spell on the sidelines under Enzo Maresca.

‌



Feyenoord took a gamble on Sterling, knowing that his lack of match fitness would mean a slow start in a new environment. The former England international grabbed his first assist in a 2-1 win over Excelsior last weekend and started De Klassieker against Ajax on Sunday.

‌



The match didn't go to plan for Feyenoord or Sterling, who managed 12 passes and zero shots in 55 minutes on the pitch before being hooked by under-pressure manager Van Persie. Feyenoord scored a late penalty to earn a 1-1 draw, but discontent is rumbling around the club, giving Van Persie little time for patience with his January signing.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

Viktor Gyokeres makes Arsenal trophy vow after stinging Carabao Cup final defeat READ MORE :

Eberechi Eze injury latest after Arsenal heartache and England squad withdrawal READ MORE :

"Those two worlds need to align sooner rather than later,” he said post-match after Sterling's fifth appearance. “We are working on that, where the most important thing is winning matches during this period of Raheem getting fitter and fitter.

‌



"So I do respect and acknowledge where he is coming from, but at the same time we have to deliver as a club.

"We have to end up second, as simple as that. I believe that he’s making steps in terms of fitness, in terms of what he delivers. At the same time, I want to see more impact off the ball, more impact on the ball.”

View 2 Images Robin van Persie is under pressure at Feyenoord (Image: Wart Brinkerhof/Marcel ter Bals/Getty Images)

‌



Feyenoord are second in the Eredivisie, but trail runaway leaders PSV Eindhoven by 15 points. They need to secure second place in order to qualify for next season's Champions League and hold a three-point lead over NEC in third and a five-point gap to fourth-placed Ajax.

Sterling is only under contract until the end of the season so has no time to waste. Before arriving in the Netherlands, he had not played a competitive match since May, when he was on loan at Arsenal.

The former Chelsea, Manchester City and Liverpool winger had plenty of options in the January transfer window, with as many as 18 clubs reportedly interested. He chose Feyenoord to play under Van Persie and try to rediscover his love of the game following a difficult period at Chelsea.

‌



"He has chosen us is because we believe in each other and that we are going to help each other in that process," Van Persie said on February. "From Raheem's point of view to be happy again and from our point of view so that he can help us and gives us a really positive boost."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Sports.title: 'Won’t put up with it' - PSG controversy erupts as manager fumes ahead of Liverpool tie
Sports.content:
'Won’t put up with it' - PSG controversy erupts as manager fumes ahead of Liverpool tie

Lens manager Pierre Sage has opposed PSG's potential bid to postpone their crucial Ligue 1 clash on April 11, which falls between the Champions League quarter-final legs against Liverpool

View 2 Images Pierre Sage has sensationally dismissed claims of PSG postponement ahead of their Champions League clash with Liverpool. (Image: Getty Images)

Lens boss Pierre Sage has dismissed suggestions that their Ligue 1 encounter with Paris Saint-Germain could be postponed ahead of their Champions League tie with Liverpool. The Reds meet the European title holders in the quarter-finals next month, and there were claims that Luis Enrique's team could have a potential title-deciding fixture against Lens moved to assist their European preparations.

‌



The Parisians are scheduled to face Lens, who trail by just a point, in a crucial top-of-the-table meeting on April 11, squeezed between the two quarter-final legs with the Reds.

‌



Following PSG's comfortable victory over Chelsea, speculation arose about whether the clash between France's leading two clubs would be rearranged to help the league leaders' bid to retain their Champions League trophy.

‌



Nevertheless, the Lens manager has expressed his opposition to the idea, with the final decision resting with the LFP (French Professional Football League).

Sage said: "We don't agree (with a postponement). There is only one date and we don't have to put up with that. We played a Coupe de France match on a Thursday and had to play against Metz on Sunday," reports the Liverpool Echo.

FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

‌



Luis Enrique delivers clear Liverpool message ahead of Champions League clash READ MORE :

"I understand that having more rest allows you to perform better, but it is a club that is performing well, who are in a lot of competitions, so they know the constraints."

Lens briefly went top this weekend following their resounding 5-1 victory over Angers, but PSG reclaimed their position at the pinnacle of French football with a 4-0 triumph away at Nice.

However, the Parisiens' match wasn't without issues as midfielders Senny Mayulu and Kang-in Lee were both compelled to leave the pitch due to injuries. The duo's fitness concerns have left PSG anxious, especially with Bradley Barcola already sidelined for several weeks due to a sprained ankle.

‌



View 2 Images Liverpool saw their Champions League hopes ended by PSG, last season. (Image: Getty Images)

The fixture, set for April 11, could be crucial in the title race as the Blood and Gold persist in their quest to clinch the league for the first time since 1998. PSG's trip to the Stade Bollaert-Delelis will occur during a critical period for them, but Lens have suffered only two defeats at home all season, securing victories in the other 14 matches.

Liverpool, on the other hand, have entered the international break once more, nursing their wounds after another lacklustre performance. The Reds succumbed to defeat at the AMEX Stadium as Danny Welbeck's brace jeopardised their Champions League aspirations.

‌



Arne Slot's team could have climbed into the top four for the first time since December had Aston Villa played on Sunday.

With victories for Manchester United and Villa, Liverpool now find themselves five points adrift of the top four in fifth – just one point ahead of Chelsea in sixth and three points clear of Brentford and Everton.
